auth_contracts.description: Return shape for `@provides(cookie_auth, ...)` skill operations. Top-level dict with `cookies: [Cookie, ...]`; each Cookie has a `name` + `value` and optional `expires` / `created` for freshness arbitration in crates/auth/src/source.rs.

required.name: domain
required.description: The cookie domain this batch is scoped to (e.g. "app.greptile.com"). The engine uses this to bind the cookies to the right host.

required.name: cookies
required.description: Array of Cookie dicts (see `cookie_fields` below for the per- element shape). Empty array is valid (means "no cookies for this domain") and causes the resolver to skip this provider.

recommended.name: count
recommended.description: Length of `cookies` — purely informational, the engine recomputes from `cookies.len()`. Useful for skill-side assertions and trace summaries.

recommended.name: source
recommended.description: Provider identifier for trace + diagnostic output (e.g. "brave-browser", "firefox", "playwright"). Helps the user tell which browser the cookies came from.

required.name: name
required.description: Cookie name. Used by the engine to construct the `name=value` pairs for the Cookie request header (crates/auth/src/source.rs::score_provider).

required.name: value
required.description: Cookie value. Joined with name as `name=value`; multiple cookies joined by `; `. Engine does not URL-decode.

recommended.name: expires
recommended.description: Unix timestamp (seconds, fractional ok) when the cookie expires. The engine filters expired cookies in `score_provider`; cookies without an `expires` are treated as session cookies and kept. `expiry` is accepted as an alias for compatibility.

recommended.name: created
recommended.description: Unix timestamp (seconds, fractional ok) when the cookie was set. Used for freshness arbitration when multiple providers return overlapping cookies — the newer one wins. Browsers like Brave/Firefox supply this; Playwright/CDP doesn't.

recommended.name: domain
recommended.description: Cookie's own domain (may differ from envelope domain on subdomains).

auth_contracts.description: Return shape for `@provides(oauth_auth, ...)` skill operations. Engine consumes via crates/auth/src/types.rs (Session::to_auth_header, auth_fields) and crates/auth/src/resolve.rs (token-refresh path).

required.name: access_token
required.description: OAuth 2.0 access token. Used by the engine to construct the Authorization: Bearer <token> header for downstream API calls. Required — missing this is the single bug that motivated this contract.

recommended.name: refresh_token
recommended.description: OAuth 2.0 refresh token. The engine refreshes automatically when the access_token is near expiry; without this the token stops working at expires_in seconds and the user must re-auth.

recommended.name: expires_in
recommended.description: Seconds until access_token expiry, per RFC 6749 §5.1. Engine derives `expires_at = now + expires_in` at store time. If omitted the engine assumes 1 hour (3600s).

recommended.name: scope
recommended.description: Space-separated list of granted scopes. Surfaced to the user via `agentos call accounts` for visibility into what the credential can do. Engine parses this into a structured `scopes` array (crates/auth/src/resolve.rs::inject_scopes_array).

recommended.name: client_id
recommended.description: OAuth client ID — required for the engine to perform token refresh on its own (without re-invoking the provider). If omitted, refresh requires re-running the provider skill.

recommended.name: token_url
recommended.description: OAuth token endpoint URL — same as client_id, required for engine-driven token refresh. If omitted, refresh round-trips through the provider skill.

ops.name: auth_store.read
ops.doc: Read the best-scored decrypted credential row for a (domain, item_type, account?) tuple.
ops.doc_full:
Read one row from the encrypted credentials store. Returns the
decrypted JSON `value` plus identifier / source / last_verified.
Domain-normalization happens on the caller side — pass the
canonical credential-store form (e.g. `".approach.app"`).

Args:
    domain: Canonical credential-store domain.
    item_type: Credential kind (`"login_credentials"`, `"cookie"`, ...).
    account: Optional identifier disambiguator.

Returns:
    `{found, identifier?, value?, source?, last_verified?}`. On
    no-match, `found` is `false` and every other field is absent.

request.name: domain
request.doc: Canonical credential-store domain.

request.name: item_type
request.doc: Credential kind (login_credentials, cookie, oauth, api_key, …).

request.name: account
request.doc: Optional identifier disambiguator.

fields.name: value
fields.doc: Decrypted JSON payload; shape depends on item_type.

fields.name: source
fields.doc: Where the row came from (onepassword, brave-browser, …).

fields.name: last_verified
fields.doc: Unix timestamp of the most recent successful verification.

ops.leading_comment:
auth_store.* ops — encrypted credential store reads.

The only SDK surface onto the encrypted credentials table. The
encryption key lives in the macOS Keychain; Python never holds it.

ops.name: capability.call
ops.doc: Match a @provides(X) skill and dispatch its tool, returning the tool's result unchanged.
ops.doc_full:
Matchmake a provider for capability `name` and dispatch `verb` on it.
The tool's return value is passed through unchanged — no envelope.

Used by SDK wrappers (`credentials.retrieve`, `llm.oneshot`, etc.)
and by skills that consume a capability across skill boundaries
without naming the provider.

Args:
    name: capability name (e.g. `"login_credentials"`, `"llm"`).
    verb: tool to invoke on the picked provider. Defaults to the
          provider's declared `via`.
    params: parameters forwarded to the picked tool, unchanged.
    skill: optional skill-id override; forces a specific provider.

Returns:
    The picked tool's return value, unchanged.

request.name: name
request.doc: Capability name to match.

request.name: verb
request.doc: Tool-name override; defaults to the provider's declared via.

request.name: params
request.doc: Parameters for the picked tool. Passed through unchanged.

request.name: skill
request.doc: Skill-id override; forces a specific provider.

response.doc: The picked tool's return value, unchanged — no envelope.

ops.name: capability.list_providers
ops.doc: List every @provides(name) provider with its ranking and credential state.
ops.doc_full:
Enumerate skills that declared `@provides(name, ...)`. Returns the
ranked candidate list without dispatching any tool — useful for
introspection, provider selection, or iterating providers with
per-provider fallback logic in skill code.

Args:
    name: capability name.
    model: optional model filter for `llm` capability matches.

Returns:
    `{providers: [{skill_id, via, cred_state, urls?}, ...]}`.

request.name: name
request.doc: Capability name to enumerate providers for.

request.name: model
request.doc: Optional model filter for llm capability matches.

ops.name: crypto.pbkdf2
ops.doc: PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA1 key derivation. Returns hex-encoded bytes.
ops.doc_full:
PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA1 key derivation. Used by browser skills to reproduce
Chrome's Safe Storage key stretch.

Args:
    password, salt: opaque strings.
    iterations: default 1.
    length: output byte length (default 16).

Returns:
    Derived key as bytes (hex-decoded by the SDK stub).

request.name: password
request.doc: Password to stretch.

request.name: salt
request.doc: Salt (opaque bytes as a string).

request.name: iterations
request.doc: Iteration count.

request.name: length
request.doc: Desired output length in bytes.

ops.leading_comment:
crypto.* ops — key derivation and symmetric decryption.

Used almost exclusively by browser skills decrypting stored cookies.
Handlers live in agentos-exec-executors.

ops.name: crypto.aes
ops.doc: AES-128-CBC decrypt. Key/data/iv are hex. Returns hex plaintext.
ops.doc_full:
AES-128-CBC decrypt. Key, ciphertext, and IV are all hex-encoded.
The returned plaintext is hex-encoded on the wire and decoded to
bytes by the SDK stub.

Args:
    key: hex (16 bytes = 32 hex chars for AES-128).
    data: ciphertext, hex.
    iv: hex. Default: 32 zero chars (Chrome convention).

Returns:
    Plaintext bytes.

request.name: key
request.doc: Key as hex (16 bytes / 32 hex chars for AES-128).

request.name: data
request.doc: Ciphertext as hex.

request.name: iv
request.doc: Initialization vector as hex. Defaults to 32 zero hex chars.

ops.leading_comment:
crypto.* ops — key derivation and symmetric decryption.

Used almost exclusively by browser skills decrypting stored cookies.
Handlers live in agentos-exec-executors.

ops.name: http.request
ops.doc: Stateless HTTP op. SDK callers use client.get/post/… — this op is their shared backend.
ops.doc_full:
Stateless HTTP op. Every verb-dispatched SDK method (`client.get`,
`client.post`, …) routes through this; skill code does not call
`http.request` directly. No cookie-jar state survives between calls —
the SDK's ambient Jar serializes cookies into the request's Cookie:
header and feeds Set-Cookie responses back into itself.

Args:
    url, method, headers, params, body, http2, timeout.

Returns:
    HttpResponse {status, body?, body_bytes?, headers, url, body_length, content_encoding}.

request.name: url
request.doc: Absolute URL.

request.name: method
request.doc: HTTP method: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH, HEAD.

request.name: headers
request.doc: Request headers. Cookies ride here under "Cookie".

request.name: params
request.doc: Query string parameters; appended to the URL.

request.name: body
request.doc: Raw request body — bytes or string, sent verbatim.

request.name: http2
request.doc: Whether to enable HTTP/2. Set false for JA4-fingerprint hosts.

ops.name: llm.resolve_tools

ops.doc_full:
Resolve a list of skill-scoped tool refs (`"skill.operation"`) into
provider-native tool definitions. Used by llm.agent loops that need
to pass the router's tool list into a chat call.

Returns:

request.name: tools

fields.name: tools

ops.name: shell.run
ops.doc: Run a binary with controlled arguments. Shell interpreters are blocked.
ops.doc_full:
Execute a binary with controlled argv. The invocation is NOT shell-parsed,
and shell interpreters (sh, bash, zsh, fish, etc.) are rejected to prevent
command-injection shortcuts.

Args:
    binary, args, timeout, cwd, input.

Returns:
    {exit_code, stdout, stderr}.

request.name: binary
request.doc: Binary to execute (absolute path or $PATH name).

request.name: args
request.doc: Argv tail — not shell-parsed.

request.name: timeout
request.doc: Timeout in seconds.

request.name: cwd
request.doc: Working directory. Defaults to the process CWD.

request.name: input
request.doc: Optional stdin bytes written to the child as UTF-8.

fields.name: exit_code
fields.doc: Exit code; -1 if the child was signalled.

ops.name: sql.query
ops.doc: Run a read-only SQL query against a named SQLite file.
ops.doc_full:
Run a read-only SQL query against a named SQLite file.

Args:
    sql: Parameterized SQL text. Use `:name` placeholders.
    db: Path to the SQLite file (`~` expands to $HOME).
    params: Named bind parameters (optional).
    attach: Alias → path map for ATTACH DATABASE (optional).

Returns:
    List of row dicts keyed by column name.

request.name: sql
request.doc: Parameterized SQL text. Use :name placeholders bound via params.

request.name: db
request.doc: Path to the SQLite file. ~ is expanded to $HOME.

request.name: params
request.doc: Named bind parameters — mapped onto :name placeholders.

request.name: attach
request.doc: Alias → path map for ATTACH DATABASE — joins across files.

response.doc: Rows — each a JSON object keyed by column name.

ops.leading_comment:
sql.* ops — SQLite query/execute against a named database file.

Handlers live in agentos-graph-executors.
